verdict.note: Verified by inspection + clean vite build: close button no longer hardcodes setSel('new'); a useEffect mirrors active's validity test (sel!=='graph' && !order.includes(sel) -> 'new'), so a closed/vanished session only bumps you to New Session if sel still points at it, else your manual switch stands. Live destructive browser close NOT run: the only sessions on :8787 are real in-flight work (closing removes their worktrees). Full browser YATU deferred pending disposable sessions.

Re-measure at HEAD 085a528 after removing the left list's '// <project> sessions' header (si-list-head div + dead .si-list-head CSS, the dead 'project' prop thread Dashboard->SessionInterface, and the orphaned session.title i18n key). Orthogonal to the close-tab-fallback contract: no tab-close button, selection state, or the event-driven useEffect fallback was touched. Prior inspection verdict still holds — close no longer hardcodes setSel('new'); the useEffect bumps to New Session only when sel still points at a vanished/closed session, else your manual switch stands. Clean vite build passes. Live destructive browser run still deferred: the only sessions on :8787 are real in-flight work whose worktrees a close would remove.

Live browser YATU (worktree vite :5190 -> backend :8787, real board). BEFORE/AFTER: on main the header action row = [⌨ nav, close(.si-act.kill)]; on this change = [⌨ nav] only (hasKill true->false). The misleading 'close' header button is GONE. Surviving close path verified live: right-click a session row -> menu shows rename+close -> clicking close raises the 'removes its worktree' confirm; cancelled with Esc (non-destructive). Destructive Pass1/Pass2 fallback landing NOT re-run — the only :8787 sessions are real in-flight work whose worktrees a real close would remove — and the event-driven fallback (useEffect bumps to New only when sel still points at a vanished session, else your switch stands) is untouched by this change; it only removed the header entry point.

Re-measure at HEAD 0d92754 after the /exit interception. Orthogonal to the close-tab-fallback contract: the new sendMsg guard only adds a NEW trigger that calls the existing act('close'); it touches neither the header Close button, act(), the close endpoint, nor the event-driven useEffect tab-fallback. Prior inspection verdict still holds — close no longer hardcodes setSel('new'); the useEffect bumps to New Session only when sel still points at a vanished session, else your manual switch stands. Clean vite build passes. Live destructive 2-session browser run still deferred: the only sessions on :8787 are real in-flight work whose worktrees a real close would remove (the /exit measurement above intercepted /close to stay non-destructive).

Orthogonal to the close-tab-fallback contract. The status-word-colour change touches only the status LABEL colour (the STATUS_COLOR map in session.js plus an inline color on the status span); it does not touch the header action row, the close action, the close endpoint, or the event-driven tab-fallback useEffect. Contract holds in spirit: closing stays row-menu-only behind a confirm, and the fallback bumps to New Session only when the selection still points at a vanished session. Clean vite build passes and the SessionWindow renders (status-word-colour filed pass with live before/after evidence). Live destructive two-session run still deferred: the only :8787 sessions are real in-flight work whose worktrees a real close would remove.

Orthogonal to the exit-closes contract. The status-word-colour change only colours the status LABEL; it does not touch the input box, the exit interception, the send-message path, or the close action. Prior pass holds in spirit. Re-run deferred because typing exit closes a live work session. Clean vite build passes.

Still orthogonal after the palette simplification. The 4-hue change touches only session.js STATUS_COLOR and the status LABEL colour; it does not touch the header action row, the close action, the close endpoint, or the event-driven tab-fallback useEffect. Contract holds: closing stays row-menu-only behind a confirm; the fallback bumps to New Session only when the selection still points at a vanished session. Clean vite build passes; SessionWindow renders. Live destructive two-session run still deferred (the only :8787 sessions are real in-flight work).

Still orthogonal after the palette simplification. The 4-hue change only colours the status LABEL; it does not touch the input box, the exit interception, the send-message path, or the close action. Prior pass holds. Re-run deferred (typing exit closes a live work session). Clean vite build passes.

Contract holds. Clean vite build passes; SessionWindow renders. Live destructive two-session run still deferred.

Still orthogonal after parked→green. One STATUS_COLOR entry, status LABEL only; does not touch the input box, the exit interception, the send-message path, or the close action. Prior pass holds. Clean vite build passes.

Orthogonal to the SessionRow face change. This change is the row HEADLINE/STATUS layout (Row 1 = tmux self-summary via sessionHeadline; Row 2 = status word + op tally) in SessionWindow.jsx + session.js. It does not touch the ❯ input box, the client-side /exit interception, the control-socket dispatch, or the close action — all in SessionInterface.jsx, untouched on this branch. Prior pass holds. Destructive live re-run deferred (the only :8787 sessions are real in-flight work; typing /exit would close one). Clean: worktree vite builds, SessionWindow renders.

Orthogonal to the SessionRow face change. The change is the row HEADLINE/STATUS layout (sessionHeadline + .sess-meta) in SessionWindow.jsx + session.js; it does not touch the header action row, the right-click Close, the close endpoint, or the event-driven tab-fallback useEffect (all SessionInterface.jsx, untouched). Contract holds: closing stays row-menu-only behind a confirm; the fallback bumps to New Session only when the selection still points at a vanished session. Destructive two-session live run still deferred (only real in-flight :8787 sessions). Clean: worktree vite builds, SessionWindow renders.

Re-verified the action-row assertion @HEAD d43cfd2: the header shows only state-driven board buttons (nav/proof/merge, now text-only + colour-coded) and NO close/kill button — closing remains solely on the row right-click menu. This change rewrote the action row from the board-command registry but did NOT touch the close action, the close endpoint, or the event-driven tab-fallback useEffect, so the fallback contract is unchanged. Live destructive two-session two-pass run still deferred (same as prior readings).

Orthogonal to this change. The board-command/button work touches the input box, the action-row buttons, and the proof overlay only — it does NOT touch session.js STATUS_COLOR (the status-word hue map) or the SessionWindow render. Prior pass (image at b4ad03e) holds; clean vite build @HEAD d43cfd2.

core invariant PASSED via real backend API: /exit keeps worktree+branch, preserves lifecycle (asking), goes offline, tmux killed, and resume relaunches it (resumable). Dashboard /-box interception + muted-grey hue verified by build+code-path, NOT a live browser run this round.

removal PASSED via real backend API: /close removes worktree+branch and drops the session off the board (the tab-removal fallback to New Session). Dashboard /-box interception + red hue verified by build+code-path, NOT a live browser run this round.

re-measured after the stopAgentProcess dedup (e55154b): /exit still keeps worktree+branch and goes offline; behaviour unchanged. Backend API proven; dashboard interception+grey hue by build+code-path, not browser.

re-measured after the stopAgentProcess dedup (e55154b): /close still removes worktree+branch+board entry; behaviour unchanged. Backend API proven; dashboard interception+red hue by build+code-path, not browser.

Restaled at HEAD only by the orthogonal campaign edits to SessionInterface.jsx — the emoji→SVG glyph swaps (AttachGlyph/BusyGlyph) and the additive harness selector; verified by git diff 5265f3f..HEAD that EVERY non-comment added line is a glyph SVG, the harness control, or a comment — no change to this scenario's asserted logic. Behavior-equivalent; prior screenshot-backed pass holds. Not re-run live: needs 2+ live tmux agent sessions in specific states, disproportionate machine load (main session shares this box) for a provably-orthogonal swap. close-tab-fallback (event-driven close → land on New or keep your switch) is driven by the tab-removal effect, untouched by glyph/harness edits.

Restaled at HEAD only by the orthogonal campaign edits to SessionInterface.jsx — the emoji→SVG glyph swaps (AttachGlyph/BusyGlyph) and the additive harness selector; verified by git diff 5265f3f..HEAD that EVERY non-comment added line is a glyph SVG, the harness control, or a comment — no change to this scenario's asserted logic. STATUS_COLOR (session.js) is BYTE-IDENTICAL since the baseline (5783a27..HEAD touched no session.js) and matches the four-hue contract exactly: working/parked→var(--green), asking/review/done→var(--yellow), error→var(--red), idle/starting/queued/close-pending/offline→var(--muted). Restaled only by the orthogonal glyph/styles edits. The status word is painted by this unchanged map; verifying the live rendered colours needs sessions in working+asking+close-pending states (no live sessions in the isolated tree). Behavior-equivalent in unchanged code; prior screenshot pass holds.

The raw-key channel (POST /api/sessions/:id/rawkey + navKeyToken encoding C-r/M-b/S-Tab/M-B/S-Up/C-c, malformed→404) is unchanged by the glyph/harness edits that restaled the node. Not re-run live: needs a live tmux pane running cat -v to read received bytes — disproporticate load for an orthogonal swap. Prior transcript-backed pass holds; the swap does not touch nav-mode key forwarding.

The reserved ⌥/⌘+I nav-mode toggle (never forwarded to tmux, not app-overridable) is in the key-handling path, untouched by the emoji→SVG/harness-selector edits that restaled the node. Behavior-equivalent; prior pass holds. Not re-run live (needs a live session + nav mode).

/exit (act('exit') — stops the agent+tmux, KEEPS the worktree → offline + relaunch) is the backend act path + the sessionCommands.js registry, untouched by the SessionInterface glyph/harness edits that restaled this node. Behavior-equivalent; prior screenshot pass holds. Not re-run live (needs a launched session to stop).

/close (act('close') — removes worktree+branch, red, the right-click Close twin) is the backend act path + registry, untouched by the glyph/harness edits. Behavior-equivalent; prior screenshot pass holds. Not re-run live (needs a launched session to remove).

Re-measured the New Session surface subset touched by the agent picker change. Browser evidence through Vite + Playwright with mocked real /api/board/config/slash/graph: opened the session console and selected New Session; .si-harness textContent is empty, aria-label is 'agent launcher', two radio buttons render as 32x28 icon-only controls with two SVGs, titles/aria-labels are Claude Code and Codex, Claude checked by default. Screenshot: /tmp/spexcode-harness-icons.png. This is NOT a full fresh pass for the scenario's create-and-poll flow; it is a focused note for the changed selector contract.

Final DOM re-measure after renaming the picker classes: Playwright opened the real dashboard shell with mocked /api/board/config/slash/graph, entered New Session, and inspected .si-agent-picker. outerHTML contains no 'harness' token, textContent is empty, aria-label is 'agent launcher', two 32x28 icon-only radio buttons render two SVG vendor marks, with title/aria-label Claude Code and Codex and Claude checked by default. Screenshot: /tmp/spexcode-agent-icons.png. Focused note for the changed picker contract, not a full create-and-poll scenario pass.

Real screenshot: console open on a selected live session. VERIFIED via DOM probe + screenshot that the header action row carries NO close/kill control (only 'New Session' and 'View Session Relationship'); closing is reachable ONLY through the row's right-click menu (rename/close, behind a confirm). NOT driven: the destructive PASS-1/PASS-2 landing behaviour (close-while-viewing → lands on New Session; close-then-switch-to-B → stays on B) — I will not close the 5 live shared sessions to measure it. Needs a throwaway session pair. Honest gap superseding the prior empty reading.

Provably orthogonal to my edit: this branch's only SessionInterface.jsx change is the searchOpen guard (early-return when the search palette modal is open) plus its prop+stateRef plumbing — the fitTextarea auto-grow/scrollbar logic is untouched, and the guard is a no-op when no palette is open (this scenario's resting condition). Not re-run live (needs 2+ live tmux agent sessions in specific states; disproportionate load on the shared box); the prior screenshot-backed pass holds.

Playwright over the live dashboard session interface (real working-session tabs). Toggle sequence, 6 assertions: nav starts OFF; single ⌘I → ON; ⌘I → OFF; single ⌥I → ON; ⌥I → OFF; ⌥⌘I (both modifiers) → stays OFF (no toggle). Screenshot shows single-⌘I nav mode engaged (yellow 'nav' badge, bottom bar '⌥/⌘+I, Esc-Esc, or click to exit'). The XOR fix (altKey!==metaKey) makes ⌥⌘I fall through un-prevent-defaulted so the browser devtools accelerator opens instead of the app swallowing it. Not driven: the /rawkey 'forwards nothing' arm (toggle branch returns before any sendRawKey by code) and literal devtools opening (headless).
